objectives: Define a game concept and translate it into a complete, testable rule set
Formulate win/lose conditions, scoring, turn structure, and edge cases
Demonstrate the rules with example playthroughs and state transitions
learning_path.title: Core Game Rules: From Idea to Formal Rule Set

objectives: Create gambling games concepts with clear player actions and outcomes
Design payout tables and risk/reward loops aligned with the rules
Demonstrate games via sample rounds and validate rule completeness
learning_path.title: Gambling Game Concepts: Probability-Driven Rule Design

objectives: Implement game rules as code using a clear game loop and state machine
Represent game state, actions, and outcomes with maintainable structures
Demonstrate games by running scripted scenarios that exercise edge cases
learning_path.title: Implementing Rules in Code: Game Loop and State

objectives: Create a playable prototype in Unity/Unreal that executes the rule set
Connect input to rule actions and display outcomes for rapid iteration
Demonstrate games by running a full loop from start to end state
learning_path.title: Engine Setup and Prototyping: Unity/Unreal Rule Prototype

objectives: Refactor rule logic into systems and components for scalability
Separate data from behavior to simplify iteration and testing
Model game entities and rule interactions using component composition
learning_path.title: Game Architecture Patterns: ECS and Component Systems for Rules

objectives: Use vectors and matrices to represent positions, directions, and transforms
Apply quaternions for stable rotations and orientation updates
Integrate math operations into gameplay logic and movement rules
learning_path.title: 3D Math Foundations for Gameplay and Simulation

objectives: Implement collision-driven interactions aligned with game rules
Configure and control rigidbody dynamics for predictable outcomes
Validate physics edge cases and ensure rules remain consistent under simulation
learning_path.title: Game Physics: Collision and Rigidbody Dynamics in Gameplay

objectives: Use the graphics pipeline to present clear game state and outcomes
Create simple shaders to communicate win/lose states and interactions
Optimize visual feedback loops to support fast player understanding
learning_path.title: Real-Time Rendering: Shaders and the Graphics Pipeline for Feedback

objectives: Profile CPU/GPU hotspots and identify performance bottlenecks
Apply targeted optimizations without changing game rules
Create a repeatable debugging workflow for gameplay and simulation issues
learning_path.title: Debugging and Profiling: Performance Optimization for Prototypes

objectives: Track rule and code changes with a clean commit history
Use branching to experiment with alternative rule sets safely
Resolve conflicts and maintain a stable mainline for demonstrations
learning_path.title: Version Control with Git: Iteration and Rule Changes

objectives: Build simple editor tools to tune rules and parameters quickly
Automate repetitive setup and testing tasks for demonstrations
Enable rapid iteration on gambling games concepts and payout tuning
learning_path.title: Scripting and Tooling: Editor Tools for Rule Tuning

objectives: Implement authoritative client-server rule execution
Replicate game state changes reliably and consistently
Demonstrate games in multiplayer with synchronized outcomes and edge-case handling
learning_path.title: Multiplayer Networking: Client-Server Rules and Replication

matched_occupation.description: Gambling games designers design innovative gambling, betting and lottery games. They determine the design, gaming rules or structure of a game. Gambling games designers may also demonstrate the game to individuals.
matched_occupation.label: gambling games designer
